etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 曷(曷) (hé) -> meaning: dry, exhausted (of water)
etymology.interpretation: The character 渴(kě, "thirsty") combines the semantic component 氵(shuǐ, "water"), essential for life and quenching thirst, with the phonetic component 曷(hé), which indicates the sound. The concept of being dry or exhausted due to a lack of water leads to the meaning of 'thirst'.

examples.chinese: 他很渴(他很渴)，所以喝了一大瓶水(所以喝了一大瓶水)。
examples.pinyin: Tā hěn kě, suǒyǐ hēle yī dà píng shuǐ.
examples.english: He was very thirsty, so he drank a big bottle of water.

examples.chinese: 运动后，我总是感到渴(運動後，我總是感到渴)。
examples.pinyin: Yùndòng hòu, wǒ zǒng shì gǎndào kě.
examples.english: After exercising, I always feel thirsty.

examples.chinese: 她渴了(她渴了)，需要喝点水(需要喝點水)。
examples.pinyin: Tā kě le, xūyào hē diǎn shuǐ.
examples.english: She is thirsty and needs to drink some water.
